title: Essay Types & Structures
description: Identify the 5 IELTS essay types and learn introduction patterns.

essay_questions.question: Zoos are inhumane and pointless. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

Many governments spend a lot of money on art. Some people think this investment in art is necessary, but others say the money is better spent on improving health and education. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.
Some people think that because of email, social media and telephones, communication among people is less personal now than it used to be. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
Many criminals re-offend after they have been punished. Why do some people continue to commit crimes after they have been punished, and what measures can be taken to tackle this problem?

In the developed world, average life expectancy is increasing. What problems will this cause for individuals and society? Suggest some measures that could be taken to reduce the impact of ageing populations.
In many countries, schools have severe problems with student behaviour. What do you think are the causes of this? What solutions can you suggest?

Some people think that it is important to use leisure time for activities that improve the mind, such as reading and doing word puzzles. Other people feel that it is important to rest the mind during leisure time. Discuss both views and give your opinion.

Many people think it is very important to protect the environment but they make no effort to do it themselves. Why do you think this is the case? What actions should individuals take to protect the environment?
Young people spend less of their free time with their family nowadays. What are the reasons for this? Are there more negative or positive sides to it?
Junk food advertising has a harmful effect on society and should be banned. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
The exploration and development of safe alternatives to fossil fuels should be the most important global priority today. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
Childhood obesity is a serious problem in many countries. What are the causes of this and how can the problem be managed?
The tendency of news media to focus on problems and emergencies rather than good news has a negative effect on individuals and society. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

Successful sports professionals can earn a great deal more money than people in other important professions. Some people think this is fully justified while others think it is unfair. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.
Some people think that computer games are bad for children, while others believe that they are useful. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of computer games.

Some governments spend a lot of money on exploring space. Some feel that this investment is justified while others think that money should be spent on public services like healthcare and education. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
Some sports are considered extremely dangerous, but many people still like them. Why do people take part in dangerous sports? Give some suggestions on how to deal with these dangers.

Despite improvements in vehicle technology, there are still large numbers of road accidents. Explain some of the causes of these accidents and suggest some measures that could be taken to address the problem.

In many countries, tourism is a major part of the economy, but it also causes environmental damage and ruins the places it exploits. It is argued that tourists should pay an additional tax to compensate for this damage. Do you agree?
Nowadays many jobs are more stressful and the working day is longer. What are the reasons for this? What can employers do to help employees?
Nowadays celebrities earn more money than politicians. What are the reasons for this? Is it a positive or negative development?

A growing number of people feel that animals should not be exploited by people while others argue that humans must employ animals to satisfy their various needs, including for food and research. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
Many governments invest a great deal of money in space research. What are the advantages and potential disadvantages of spending money on space exploration?
There is an increasing trend around the world to have a small family rather than a large family. What are some of the advantages and disadvantages of having a small family rather than a large family?
Computers are being used more in education nowadays than in the past. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of this trend.
Some people feel that playing computer games has a negative impact on children's health and social skills. Others say that it can have a positive impact. Discuss both these views.

Some people think that the best way to reduce crime is to give longer prison sentences. Others, however, believe there are better alternative ways of reducing crime. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
Nowadays online shopping is rapidly replacing physical shopping in stores. Do the benefits outweigh the drawbacks, in your view?

Homeschooling is becoming increasingly popular as more and more parents are choosing to educate their children in this way. Why do you think parents might choose homeschooling rather than traditional schools? Do you think homeschooling is more beneficial?
Nowadays people use social media to keep up with the news. Why do they do this? Is it a positive or negative trend?
Because of the rising cost of housing, many young people are increasingly being forced to live with their parents into their 30s. Do the advantages of this trend outweigh the disadvantages?
Many museums charge admission fees while others are free. Do the advantages of charging people to visit a museum outweigh the disadvantages?
Some people believe that it is wrong to keep animals in zoos, while others think that zoos are important for education and conservation. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
Thanks to the development of technology, many employers are now offering employees the option to work from home. Is this a positive or negative development?
Stress is now a major problem in many countries around the world. What are some of the factors in modern society that cause this stress, and how can we reduce it?
Many governments invest a great deal of money in space research. What are the advantages and potential disadvantages of spending money on space exploration?

Many people believe that family has the greatest influence on a child’s development, while others argue that other factors such as television, friends and music are more significant. Discuss both views and give your opinion.

essay_type_sort.phrase: In this essay, I will [verb] ... and [verb] ...
essay_type_sort.tip: The TEMPLATE 'In this essay, I will...' is universal — but what comes AFTER changes by essay type. Opinion: '...explain why I agree.' Discussion: '...consider both views.' P-S: '...outline causes and suggest solutions.' A-D: '...outline advantages and drawbacks.'

essay_type_sort.phrase: In this essay, I will consider both views and explain why I agree that...
essay_type_sort.tip: 'Both views' = Discussion essay ONLY. This phrase does two jobs: previews the two-sided structure AND hints at your opinion. In an Opinion essay, you don't present 'both views' — you argue YOUR side. In P-S, you discuss problems and solutions, not views.

essay_type_sort.phrase: Those who support [X] usually argue that...
essay_type_sort.tip: Reporter language is ONLY for Discussion essays. You are reporting OTHER people's arguments neutrally. In Opinion essays, you state YOUR view directly. In P-S and A-D, you describe problems/advantages objectively — no 'arguing' involved.

The Umbrella phrase works everywhere — any time you need to introduce two ideas in one paragraph. Just change the noun: 'reasons' (Opinion/Discussion), 'causes' (P-S), 'benefits/drawbacks' (A-D), 'factors' (Two-Part).

essay_type_sort.phrase: Others, however, myself included, feel that...
essay_type_sort.tip: The 'myself included' switch is unique to Discussion essays — it marks the shift from Reporter mode (BP1) to Judge mode (BP2). No other essay type uses this Reporter→Judge transition.

essay_type_sort.phrase: Challenging though this problem is, there is much that can be done.
essay_type_sort.tip: This concessive solution opener is specific to Problem-Solution structures. It acknowledges the problem ('Challenging though...') then pivots to solutions ('...can be done'). Two-Part works if one part asks for solutions. You would NOT use this in Opinion, Discussion, or A-D.

'Indeed' is the most universal evidence introducer. It means 'in fact / truly' (NOT 'for example'). It works in every essay type, every paragraph position where you present evidence. [F:10+] — the most frequent examiner phrase in the entire corpus.
'Clearly' is universal — it closes ANY paragraph by confirming what the paragraph proved. It's the examiner's favourite Link word. [F:8+] across all essay types.

essay_type_sort.phrase: There are, however, some drawbacks associated with...
essay_type_sort.tip: This transition specifically shifts from advantages to disadvantages. The word 'drawbacks' signals an A-D structure. Not used in Opinion (you argue one side), Discussion (you present views, not advantages), or P-S (you present problems, not drawbacks). Two-Part ONLY if the question specifically asks for advantages and disadvantages.

essay_type_sort.phrase: Despite these benefits, there are also serious negative outcomes...
essay_type_sort.tip: Another advantages→disadvantages transition. The pattern 'Despite these [positives], [negatives]' is the signature move of an A-D essay. It would sound odd in any other essay type because it frames the discussion as 'benefits vs. negatives.'

Opinion essays need YOUR position throughout. Discussion essays need your opinion in BP2/BP3 or the conclusion. Problem-Solution and pure Adv-Disadv stay OBJECTIVE — no personal opinion needed. ⚠️ Exception: A-D 'Outweigh' questions DO need 'In my view' because they ask for evaluation. Two-Part: only if one question asks for your opinion.

essay_type_sort.phrase: While some believe ..., others feel that ...
essay_type_sort.tip: This 'two sides' Background pattern is for Opinion and Discussion essays — both present a debate between views. Problem-Solution and A-D essays use a DIFFERENT Background: 'This has led to growing concerns about...' or 'One of the results of this has been...' They introduce a problem/situation, NOT two opposing views.

essay_type_sort.phrase: Efforts should instead be made to...
essay_type_sort.tip: Recommendations suit Opinion (asserting what should change) and Problem-Solution (proposing action — this is the natural conclusion for P-S). Discussion essays end with a balanced judgement, not a strong recommendation. A-D conclusions weigh the two sides. Two-Part: only if one question asks for solutions.
The most universal additive connector. Works as glue in any paragraph, any essay type. It replaces the B1 habit 'Also, ...' with proper academic cohesion. [F:7+]

essay_type_sort.phrase: In this essay, I will outline [the causes] and suggest [solutions].
essay_type_sort.tip: This thesis pattern previews BOTH halves of a Problem-Solution essay: causes/problems in BP1, solutions in BP2. It is NOT used in Opinion or Discussion (which don't discuss 'causes and solutions'). Two-Part works if the question has a causes+solutions structure.

essay_type_sort.phrase: This essay will discuss the main advantages and potential drawbacks of...
essay_type_sort.tip: This thesis previews the two halves of an Advantages-Disadvantages essay. Notice: no 'I believe' — pure A-D essays stay objective. The phrase 'advantages and drawbacks' signals A-D structure specifically. It would NOT fit in any other essay type.
